title: Wireless Protocol Landscape
subtitle: Protocols Powering Cisco Wireless LAN Networks

protocols.name: CAPWAP
protocols.desc: Control and Provisioning of Wireless Access Points. Used between Access Points and the Wireless LAN Controller.
details: Creates secure tunnels between AP and WLC.
Used for AP registration and management.
Standard protocol for Cisco lightweight AP deployments.

protocols.name: IEEE 802.11
protocols.desc: Core wireless communication standard used for Wi-Fi connectivity between clients and access points.
details: Supports 2.4 GHz, 5 GHz and 6 GHz bands.
Forms the foundation of modern WLANs.

protocols.name: WPA2 / WPA3
protocols.desc: Provides wireless encryption, authentication and secure client access.
details: WPA3 offers stronger security than WPA2.

protocols.name: RADIUS
protocols.desc: Used for centralized Authentication, Authorization and Accounting services.

protocols.name: DHCP
protocols.desc: Assigns IP addresses and network parameters to wireless clients and access points.
details: Provides subnet mask and gateway information.

protocols.name: DNS
protocols.desc: Supports hostname resolution and WLC discovery mechanisms.
details: Supports WLC discovery using DNS records.
Used by wireless clients and controllers.
Reduces dependency on manual IP addressing.
